welcome.title: Registered for Hejje Gala
welcome.subtitle: Bengaluru's Corporate Active Mobility Challenge
welcome.description: Hejje Gala is a city-wide active mobility challenge that encourages Bengaluru corporates to promote walking and cycling either as primary commute modes or as first- and last-mile links to public transport—fostering healthier, greener commuting through friendly, measurable competition.

mobilityBills.title: Shaping the Future
mobilityBills.facilitiesDescription: 10 Bengaluru metro stations and 11 BMTC, TTMC stations have a basic provision of safe cycle parking and an integrated bicycle repair kiosk close to the station premises, sometimes inside the paid parking area. The metro stations also have walking facilities which work well in some and not so well in others. The goal is to increase the first mile/last mile to the metro and demand better cycling & walking facilities be extended to all metro stations.
mobilityBills.planningDescription: The 2020 Comprehensive Mobility Plan finalised by the Directorate of Urban Land Transport along with BMRCL has planned for 974 kms of footpath to be constructed with 600 kms of cycle track and 103 kms of Tender Sure Roads. In order to make these plans implementable, we will use the challenge as an opportunity to drive these conversations further.
mobilityBills.policyDescription: The Active Mobility Bill, drafted by DULT (Urban Development Department), provides a framework for Karnataka cities—especially those above 50,000 population—to promote walking and cycling. It proposes dedicated active mobility cells to enable budgeting, infrastructure delivery, and maintenance, and includes citizen audits and grievance redressal mechanisms.

Hejje Gala is a city-wide active mobility challenge that encourages Bengaluru corporates to promote walking and cycling either as primary commute.
about.corporationDescription: This corporation is committed to promoting active mobility and sustainability. Through the Hejje Gala Challenge, we encourage our employees to adopt healthier commuting habits and reduce carbon emissions.
about.companyDescription: This company is dedicated to creating a sustainable future through active mobility. Our employees actively participate in walking and cycling activities, contributing to environmental conservation and personal well-being.

whyHejjeGala.supportShiftTitle: Support Bengaluru's shift to sustainable mobility

faqs.question: What is Hejje Gala?
faqs.answer: Hejje Gala is a city-wide corporate active mobility challenge in Bengaluru that encourages employees to adopt walking and cycling as part of their daily commute. The challenge invites corporate organisations to officially participate and motivate their workforce to choose healthier and more sustainable ways of travelling to work.

faqs.question: What does "active mobility" mean in the context of this challenge?
faqs.answer: Active mobility refers to walking and cycling, either as a primary mode of travel or as a first- and/or last-mile connection to public transport such as the metro or bus.

faqs.question: Who is the challenge for?
faqs.answer: Hejje Gala is designed for corporate organisations and their employees in Bengaluru.

faqs.question: How do organisations participate in Hejje Gala?
faqs.answer: Organisations participate by officially registering for the challenge and encouraging their employees to take part. Participation is organisation-led, with companies playing a key role in promoting active commuting within their workplace.

faqs.question: Can individual employees participate without their organisation?
faqs.answer: Hejje Gala is a corporate challenge, and participation will be through registered organisations. Employees can only participate as part of their company's team and not individually.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the challenge?
How is participation tracked?
faqs.answer: Employee participation is tracked through a digital platform that records walking and cycling activity. Participating organisations can view dashboards and leaderboards that show overall participation, kilometres covered, and aggregated impact.

faqs.question: What kind of data is measured?
faqs.textAfter: All data is presented at an aggregated level for organisations.

faqs.question: Are there winners or recognitions?
faqs.answer: Yes. Organisations demonstrating strong participation and impact will be recognised and publicly acknowledged for their leadership in promoting active mobility and sustainable commuting in Bengaluru.

faqs.question: Does Hejje Gala involve building or redesigning streets or infrastructure?
faqs.answer: No. Hejje Gala focuses on behaviour change, workplace engagement, and active commuting practices. It does not involve street design or infrastructure implementation.

faqs.question: How can my organisation register?
faqs.answer: Organisations can register through the Hejje Gala website by clicking the "Register Now" button and completing the organisation sign-up process.

faqs.question: Who can we contact for more information?
faqs.answer: For queries related to participation, partnerships, or the challenge format, please reach out to [name + email address] or [name + email address].
